easy.q: What surprise attack brought the United States into World War II in December 1941?
Who was the British Prime Minister who led the UK through most of World War II?
Who was the leader of Nazi Germany during World War II?
What was the code name for the Allied invasion of Normandy on June 6, 1944?
Which two atomic bombs were dropped on Japan to end the Pacific War?
What were the two main alliances in World War II?
Who was the U.S. President when Japan attacked Pearl Harbor?
In what year did World War II begin?
In what year did World War II end?
Germany's rapid military advance strategy in World War II was called what?
What country did Germany invade to start World War II in Europe?
Who was the Supreme Allied Commander in Europe during the D-Day invasion?
What was the Holocaust?
easy.a: The systematic genocide of six million Jews (and millions of others) by Nazi Germany

easy.q: Which country's surrender in September 1945 officially ended World War II?
What iconic 'Rosie the Riveter' poster represented during the war?
easy.a: Women working in wartime factories on the home front

easy.q: What was the name of Hitler's secret police in Nazi Germany?
Who was the leader of the Soviet Union during World War II?
Which Pacific island-hopping campaign aimed to get U.S. forces close enough to bomb Japan?
What was the name of the German air force in World War II?
The Allied powers included the United States, the United Kingdom, and which other major power?
What did the letters 'VE Day' stand for?
What letter designation identified American fighter pilot volunteers who flew for China before the U.S. entered the war?
Which French general led the Free French Forces in exile?
What was rationing on the home front?
easy.a: Government limits on civilians' purchases of scarce goods (food, fuel, rubber, etc.)

easy.q: Germany, Italy, and Japan formed what pact making them the main Axis powers?
What famous speech by Churchill included the line 'We shall fight on the beaches'?
easy.a: His 1940 speech to the House of Commons after the Dunkirk evacuation

easy.q: What was the name of the wall of German anti-invasion fortifications along the Atlantic coast?
Who was Italy's fascist dictator during most of World War II?
What was VJ Day?
easy.a: Victory over Japan Day, marking Japan's surrender (August 15/September 2, 1945)

easy.q: What wartime slogan warned Americans that 'Loose Lips' do what?
The Normandy landings took place on which beaches in France?
easy.a: Utah, Omaha, Gold, Juno, and Sword

easy.q: What machine did Alan Turing help crack to decode German secret messages?
What was the name of the evacuated Allied soldiers' rescue operation from the beaches of Dunkirk in 1940?
Which American general was nicknamed 'Old Blood and Guts'?
What was the Manhattan Project?
easy.a: The secret U.S. program to develop the atomic bomb

easy.q: Which major sea battle in 1942 turned the tide against Japan in the Pacific?
What was the name of Germany's invasion of the Soviet Union in 1941?
Which country was divided into occupation zones after Germany's defeat?
What famous propaganda image showed a sailor kissing a nurse in Times Square on VJ Day?
easy.a: The V-J Day in Times Square photograph (by Alfred Eisenstaedt)

easy.q: What branch of the U.S. military was the primary force in the Pacific island campaigns?
easy.a: The U.S. Marine Corps (and U.S. Navy / Army)

easy.q: Which battle was the largest tank battle in history, fought on the Eastern Front in 1943?
What was the name of the main Nazi concentration and extermination camp complex in Poland?
What does 'SS' stand for in the context of Nazi Germany?
Which Allied conference in 1945 divided post-war Europe between the Western powers and the Soviet Union?
What was the name of Japan's surprise aerial attack force that flew suicide missions?
In the European theater, what were the three main Allied nations sometimes called?
Where was Adolf Hitler's mountain retreat in Bavaria?
easy.a: The Berghof (on the Obersalzberg near Berchtesgaden)

easy.q: What year did the United States declare war on Japan after Pearl Harbor?
What popular slogan encouraged Americans on the home front to conserve resources during World War II?
easy.a: Use it up, wear it out, make it do, or do without

easy.q: What did FDR mean when he called December 7, 1941 'a date which will live in infamy'?
easy.a: It was the day Japan attacked Pearl Harbor, drawing the U.S. into World War II

easy.q: What was the name for the massive German Christmas offensive in December 1944?
What item of clothing did the Nazis force Jewish people to wear for identification?
What was the war in North Africa primarily fought over?
easy.a: Control of the Suez Canal and North African supply routes

easy.q: Who led the German Afrika Korps in North Africa and was called the 'Desert Fox'?
Which Pacific island battle is famous for the flag-raising photograph on Mount Suribachi?

medium.q: What was the code name for the Allied invasion of North Africa in November 1942?
medium.a: Operation Torch

medium.q: Which German field marshal surrendered at Stalingrad in February 1943?
medium.a: Field Marshal Friedrich Paulus

medium.q: What was the Lend-Lease Act, passed by the U.S. in 1941?
medium.a: A program that supplied Allied nations with war materials before the U.S. officially entered the war

medium.q: The Wannsee Conference of January 1942 coordinated what Nazi plan?
medium.a: The 'Final Solution' — the systematic extermination of European Jews

medium.q: What was the name of Germany's strategic bombing campaign against British cities in 1940–41?
medium.a: The Blitz

medium.q: Which Pacific naval battle in May 1942 was the first fought entirely by aircraft — no ships fired on each other?
medium.a: The Battle of the Coral Sea

medium.q: What was the code name for the Allied invasion of Sicily in July 1943?
medium.a: Operation Husky

medium.q: What executive order authorized the internment of Japanese Americans during the war?
medium.a: Executive Order 9066 (signed by FDR in February 1942)

medium.q: Who commanded the British 8th Army that defeated Rommel at El Alamein?
medium.a: General Bernard Montgomery

medium.q: What was the name of Hitler's secret headquarters in East Prussia?
medium.a: The Wolf's Lair (Wolfsschanze)

medium.q: Which German battleship was sunk by the Royal Navy in May 1941 after just one major sortie?
medium.a: The Bismarck

medium.q: What was Operation Market Garden, launched in September 1944?
medium.a: A failed Allied airborne operation to seize bridges in the Netherlands and push into Germany

medium.q: The Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor sank or damaged how many U.S. battleships?
medium.a: Eight battleships

medium.q: What was the name of the U.S. Navy's codebreaking unit that was key to the Midway victory?
medium.a: Station HYPO (Combat Intelligence Unit, Pearl Harbor)

medium.q: What did 'GI' stand for in the slang used to describe American soldiers?
medium.a: Government Issue (or General Issue)

medium.q: The Doolittle Raid of April 1942 saw U.S. bombers launched from a carrier to bomb which city?
medium.a: Tokyo (and other Japanese cities)

medium.q: What was the Red Ball Express?
medium.a: A trucking convoy system that supplied Allied forces across France after D-Day

medium.q: How did the German U-boat campaign threaten the Allies?
medium.a: By sinking Allied supply ships in the Atlantic, disrupting the flow of men and materiel to Britain

medium.q: What was the name of the top-secret facility in New Mexico where the atomic bomb was designed and built?
medium.a: Los Alamos (Laboratory)

medium.q: Who was the commander of Allied forces in the Pacific (Southwest Pacific Area)?
medium.a: General Douglas MacArthur

medium.q: What was the Nuremberg Trials?
medium.a: Post-war military tribunals that prosecuted Nazi war criminals for crimes against humanity

medium.q: Which country did Germany, Italy, and Japan sign the Tripartite Pact in September 1940 in?
medium.a: Berlin (the pact was signed in Berlin)

medium.q: What was the name of the Japanese naval commander who planned the Pearl Harbor attack?
medium.a: Admiral Isoroku Yamamoto

medium.q: The liberation of Paris in August 1944 was led primarily by which Free French force?
medium.a: The French 2nd Armored Division (under General Leclerc)

medium.q: What was the purpose of the Women's Army Corps (WAC) in the U.S.?
medium.a: To allow women to serve in non-combat military roles, freeing men for combat

medium.q: Which Italian city hosted the signing of the Italian armistice in September 1943?
medium.a: Cassibile, Sicily (the armistice was signed there)

medium.q: What was the Battle of Britain?
medium.a: The 1940 air campaign in which the RAF defeated the Luftwaffe's attempt to gain air superiority over Britain

medium.q: What did the Nuremberg Laws of 1935 do?
medium.a: Stripped German Jews of citizenship and banned marriage between Jews and non-Jews

medium.q: Which Axis ally switched sides and declared war on Germany in October 1943?
medium.a: Italy

medium.q: What was the name of the German plan to invade France through the Ardennes forest in 1940?
medium.a: Fall Gelb (Case Yellow)

medium.q: Which Pacific island battle lasted 36 days and resulted in the highest U.S. Marine casualties of the Pacific war?
medium.a: The Battle of Iwo Jima

medium.q: What city was the site of the decisive Soviet victory that turned the tide on the Eastern Front?
medium.a: Stalingrad

medium.q: The 'Phoney War' refers to the period of inactivity in Western Europe between September 1939 and when?
medium.a: May 1940 (when Germany launched its offensive in the West)

medium.q: Who led the famous Doolittle Raid on Japan?
medium.a: Lieutenant Colonel Jimmy Doolittle

medium.q: What was Operation Barbarossa's most significant strategic failure?
medium.a: Germany failed to capture Moscow or knock the Soviet Union out of the war before winter 1941

medium.q: What was the role of the OSS in World War II?
medium.a: The Office of Strategic Services was the U.S. wartime intelligence and special operations agency

medium.q: Which battle in the Philippines in 1944 was the largest naval battle in history?
medium.a: The Battle of Leyte Gulf

medium.q: What was a 'war bond' and why did governments sell them?
medium.a: Loans from citizens to the government to fund the war effort, sold with patriotic campaigns

medium.q: What was the name of the Allied supply route that ran from Iran through the Soviet Union to deliver war materials?
medium.a: The Persian Corridor (also called the Persian Gulf Command supply route)

medium.q: What cargo was the Liberty Ships designed to carry across the Atlantic?
medium.a: War supplies, troops, food, and equipment to Allied forces

medium.q: Which Japanese city was the target of the first atomic bomb on August 6, 1945?
medium.a: Hiroshima

medium.q: Who commanded U.S. naval forces during the Battle of Midway?
medium.a: Admiral Chester Nimitz (with Rear Admirals Fletcher and Spruance in tactical command)

medium.q: What was the Resistance in occupied France?
medium.a: An underground movement of French civilians and fighters who sabotaged the Nazi occupation

medium.q: What was the name of the German army group that suffered catastrophic defeat at Stalingrad?
medium.a: Army Group B (specifically the German 6th Army)

medium.q: What conference in November 1943 saw the first meeting of Roosevelt, Churchill, and Stalin?
medium.a: The Tehran Conference

medium.q: What was the 'final solution to the Jewish question' officially known as in German?
medium.a: Endlösung der Judenfrage

medium.q: Which island did the U.S. capture in August 1942 in the first major Allied offensive of the Pacific war?
medium.a: Guadalcanal

medium.q: What was the name of the German defensive line in Italy that held up Allied advance in 1943–44?
medium.a: The Gustav Line (Winter Line)

medium.q: What was the 'Arsenal of Democracy' speech by FDR calling for?
medium.a: For the U.S. to massively increase war production to supply the Allies

medium.q: What was the August 1939 pact between Germany and the Soviet Union that shocked the world?
medium.a: The Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact (Nazi-Soviet Non-Aggression Pact)

medium.q: What was the Tuskegee Airmen?
medium.a: The first African American military aviators in the U.S. armed forces, who flew in WWII

medium.q: What was the name of the Allied operation to liberate Rome, launched in January 1944?
medium.a: Operation Shingle (the Anzio landings)

medium.q: In which year did France fall to Germany?
Who was the U.S. commander of the Central Pacific theater and the naval drive toward Japan?
medium.a: Admiral Chester W. Nimitz

medium.q: What was the Dunkirk evacuation of May–June 1940?
medium.a: The emergency rescue of over 330,000 Allied troops from the beaches of Dunkirk, France

hard.q: What was the exact date of D-Day, the Allied invasion of Normandy?
hard.a: June 6, 1944

hard.q: Operation Overlord was the overall plan for the liberation of Europe. What was the specific code name for the naval portion of D-Day?
hard.a: Operation Neptune

hard.q: What was the code name for the German plan to invade Britain, planned but never executed?
hard.a: Operation Sea Lion (Unternehmen Seelöwe)

hard.q: The July 20, 1944 assassination attempt on Hitler is known by what name?
hard.a: Operation Valkyrie (the July 20 Plot)

hard.q: Who led the July 20, 1944 bomb plot against Hitler?
hard.a: Colonel Claus von Stauffenberg

hard.q: What was the Baedeker Blitz?
hard.a: German retaliatory bombing raids in 1942 targeting English cultural and historic cities (Bath, Exeter, etc.)

hard.q: What date did Japan formally surrender aboard the USS Missouri, ending World War II?
hard.a: September 2, 1945

hard.q: What was the name of the bomber that dropped the atomic bomb on Hiroshima?
hard.a: The Enola Gay (a B-29 Superfortress)

hard.q: Who piloted the Enola Gay on the Hiroshima mission?
hard.a: Colonel Paul Tibbets

hard.q: What was the name of the second atomic bomb dropped on Nagasaki on August 9, 1945?
hard.a: Fat Man (the Hiroshima bomb was 'Little Boy')

hard.q: Which German general was known as 'Smiling Albert' and commanded Army Group B during the Battle of the Bulge?
hard.a: Field Marshal Walter Model

hard.q: What was Operation Barbarossa's original start date, delayed by the Balkan campaign?
hard.a: Originally planned for May 1941; delayed to June 22, 1941

hard.q: What was the 'Butcher of Lyon,' the SS officer responsible for deporting thousands of French Jews?
hard.a: Klaus Barbie

hard.q: In the Battle of Midway, how many Japanese aircraft carriers were sunk?
hard.a: Four (Akagi, Kaga, Sōryū, and Hiryū)

hard.q: What was the ULTRA secret?
hard.a: The Allied intelligence operation that decoded German Enigma communications at Bletchley Park

hard.q: What was the Malmedy Massacre during the Battle of the Bulge?
hard.a: The execution of approximately 84 American POWs by the SS Leibstandarte on December 17, 1944

hard.q: What Japanese code name referred to the attack plan on Pearl Harbor?
hard.a: Operation Z (also called Operation AI in combined form)

hard.q: Which U.S. battleship was the only one to get underway during the attack on Pearl Harbor, before being run aground to avoid blocking the harbor entrance?
hard.a: USS Nevada

hard.q: What was the 'Hunger Winter' (Hongerwinter) of 1944–45?
hard.a: A famine in German-occupied Netherlands that killed over 20,000 civilians

hard.q: What was the role of the Navajo Code Talkers in the Pacific war?
hard.a: Native American Marines who used the Navajo language as an unbreakable military communications code

hard.q: Which SS unit carried out the Babi Yar massacre in September 1941, killing over 33,000 Jews in two days?
hard.a: Einsatzgruppe C (specifically Sonderkommando 4a)

hard.q: What was the name of the top-secret Allied deception operation designed to mislead Germany about the D-Day landing site?
hard.a: Operation Fortitude (part of Operation Bodyguard)

hard.q: General Anthony McAuliffe famously replied 'Nuts!' to a German surrender demand during which battle?
hard.a: The Battle of the Bulge (specifically the siege of Bastogne)

hard.q: What date did Germany invade Poland, starting World War II in Europe?
hard.a: September 1, 1939

hard.q: What was the Japanese military policy of 'bushido' and how did it affect POW treatment?
hard.a: The warrior code emphasizing death over surrender led to brutal treatment of Allied prisoners

hard.q: Which German officer commanded the panzer forces that executed the breakthrough at Sedan in May 1940?
hard.a: General Heinz Guderian

hard.q: What was the 'Ghost Army' (23rd Headquarters Special Troops) in World War II?
hard.a: A secret U.S. Army unit that used inflatable tanks, fake radio signals, and sound effects to deceive the Germans

hard.q: What was the date that Germany surrendered, ending the war in Europe?
hard.a: May 8, 1945 (VE Day)

hard.q: What was the exact name of the conference where the 'Big Three' agreed to divide post-war Germany?
hard.a: The Potsdam Conference (July–August 1945)

hard.q: Which Soviet Marshal commanded the final assault on Berlin in April–May 1945?
hard.a: Marshal Georgy Zhukov (1st Belorussian Front), with Konev's 1st Ukrainian Front

hard.q: What was the Bataan Death March?
hard.a: The forced 65-mile march of approximately 75,000 American and Filipino POWs by Japanese forces in April 1942

hard.q: What was the name of the German pocket battleship sunk by her own crew after the Battle of the River Plate?
hard.a: The Admiral Graf Spee (scuttled off Montevideo, December 1939)

hard.q: What was the 'Magic' intelligence program?
hard.a: The U.S. effort to decode Japanese diplomatic and military communications (JN-25 cipher)

hard.q: What was Operation Anthropoid?
hard.a: The 1942 assassination of SS General Reinhard Heydrich in Prague by Czech paratroopers

hard.q: In what month and year did the U.S. begin the liberation of the Philippines?
hard.a: October 1944 (MacArthur landed at Leyte on October 20)

hard.q: What German city was firebombed in February 1945, causing enormous civilian casualties in a controversial Allied raid?
hard.a: Dresden

hard.q: Which Japanese admiral was killed when his plane was ambushed by U.S. fighters in April 1943?
hard.a: Admiral Isoroku Yamamoto

hard.q: What was the 'Bridge Too Far' that the Allies failed to capture in Operation Market Garden?
hard.a: The Arnhem bridge over the Rhine in the Netherlands

hard.q: What was the name of the German 1944 offensive at the Battle of the Bulge?
hard.a: Operation Wacht am Rhein (Watch on the Rhine)

hard.q: What was the significance of the battle of Khalkhin Gol (Nomonhan) in 1939?
hard.a: A decisive Soviet victory over Japan that convinced Japan to expand south into the Pacific rather than attack the USSR

hard.q: Who was the American scientist who directed the Los Alamos laboratory and is called the 'father of the atomic bomb'?
hard.a: J. Robert Oppenheimer

hard.q: What was the 'Murmansk run' during World War II?
hard.a: The dangerous Allied convoy route supplying the Soviet Union through the Arctic Ocean to Murmansk

hard.q: What was the 'Tripartite Pact' year of signing?
hard.a: September 27, 1940

hard.q: Which Japanese general ordered the Rape of Nanking in December 1937 (preceding the official war start)?
hard.a: General Iwane Matsui (commander of the Central China Area Army)

hard.q: What was the 'Hump' that U.S. air forces flew to supply China?
hard.a: The eastern Himalayan mountain range, flown to supply Nationalist China after the Burma Road was cut

hard.q: What was the name of the U.S. Army division known as the 'Screaming Eagles' that held Bastogne?
hard.a: The 101st Airborne Division

hard.q: Which German general surrendered the German 6th Army at Stalingrad on February 2, 1943?
hard.a: Field Marshal Friedrich Paulus (promoted to Field Marshal just before the surrender)

hard.q: What was Operation Torch's primary strategic purpose?
hard.a: To establish a second front in North Africa and relieve pressure on the Soviet Union while training Allied forces

hard.q: What was the code name for the German offensive in the Ardennes in December 1944?
hard.a: Unternehmen Herbstnebel (Operation Autumn Mist), commonly called the Battle of the Bulge

hard.q: What was the name of the British commando raid in August 1942 on the French port of Dieppe, which resulted in catastrophic Allied casualties?
hard.a: The Dieppe Raid (Operation Jubilee)

hard.q: What year did the Soviet Union sign a neutrality pact with Japan, and why was it strategically important?
hard.a: April 1941; it freed Soviet forces in the east to defend against Germany, and Japan to move south

hard.q: The 'Dambusters Raid' (Operation Chastise) in May 1943 used what special weapon?
hard.a: Bouncing bombs (Upkeep mines) designed by Barnes Wallis, to breach the Ruhr dams

hard.q: What was the 'Pact of Steel' signed in May 1939?
hard.a: A military alliance between Nazi Germany and Fascist Italy signed before the formal Axis Tripartite Pact

hard.q: Who was the U.S. Secretary of War who oversaw the Manhattan Project and atomic bomb decision?
hard.a: Henry L. Stimson

hard.q: What was the name of the massive Soviet offensive in June 1944 that destroyed Army Group Centre?
hard.a: Operation Bagration
